title: The Long-Run Effects of the Slave Trade on Economic Development in Africa

sections.title: Historical Background: The African Slave Trades
sections.label: sec:history

tables.caption: Appendix: Historical Slave Trade Data Validation
tables.label: tab:appendix-historical

qa.question: How many total slaves were exported across all trade routes?
qa.answer: 11,796 thousand

qa.question: What fraction of countries have positive Atlantic slave exports?
qa.answer: 41 out of 52 countries (79 percent)

qa.question: What is the mean log GDP per capita (PPP, 2000) for the sample?
How many African countries are in the sample?
What is the mean ethnic fractionalization index?
What is the mean trust level from the World Values Survey?

tables.label: tab:ols-slave-gdp

qa.question: What is the OLS coefficient on log slaves exported in column 1?
What is the R-squared in column 4?
What is the coefficient on absolute latitude in column 1?

tables.caption: IV First Stage: Distance to Coast as Instrument
tables.label: tab:iv-first-stage

qa.question: What is the first-stage F-statistic on excluded instruments in column 1?
What is the first-stage coefficient on log distance to coast in column 1?
Is the instrument strong (F > 10)?
qa.answer: Yes, all first-stage F-statistics exceed 72

tables.caption: IV Second Stage: Slave Trade and Economic Development
tables.label: tab:iv-second-stage

qa.question: What is the 2SLS coefficient on log slaves exported in column 1?
What is the Hansen J overidentification p-value in column 2?
What is the LIML coefficient on log slaves exported?
Is the IV coefficient larger or smaller in magnitude than the OLS?
qa.answer: Larger in magnitude: IV is -0.318 vs OLS -0.214, consistent with attenuation bias in OLS

qa.question: What is the IV coefficient on slave trade intensity for log GDP per capita?
What is the OLS coefficient on slave trade for the trust outcome?
Does the effect persist when measuring trade only through 1800?
qa.answer: Yes, coefficient is -0.248 (column 3)

tables.caption: Mechanisms: Slave Trade and Ethnic Fractionalization
tables.label: tab:mechanisms-ethnic-frac

qa.question: What is the IV coefficient on log slaves exported for ethnic fractionalization?
What is the OLS coefficient on slave trade for ethnic fractionalization in column 1?
Is the IV coefficient larger than the OLS coefficient?
qa.answer: Yes, IV (0.121) > OLS (0.084), suggesting attenuation bias in OLS

tables.caption: Mechanisms: Slave Trade, Ethnic Fractionalization, and Trust
tables.label: tab:mechanisms-trust

What is the coefficient on ethnic fractionalization in the full chain model (column 3)?

tables.caption: Falsification Tests: Geographic Predictors of Slave Trade
tables.label: tab:falsification-geography

qa.question: Is the slave trade interaction with malaria index statistically significant?
qa.answer: No, coefficient is -0.008 with SE (0.012)

qa.question: Does the main effect of slave trade remain significant after adding geographic interactions?
qa.answer: Yes, the main effect remains around -0.20 and statistically significant in all columns

tables.caption: Robustness: Progressive Controls and Alternative Samples
tables.label: tab:robustness-progressive

qa.question: What is the baseline 2SLS coefficient on slave trade?
Does the slave trade coefficient remain significant when adding settler mortality?
qa.answer: Yes, coefficient is -0.284 with SE (0.061)

qa.question: What is the sample size with settler mortality controls?
qa.answer: 49 countries

equations.label: eq:persistence
equations.description: Main persistence equation: current income $\log y_i$ on normalized slave exports $\log S_i$ and geographic controls $X_i$.

qa.question: What is the null hypothesis of no persistence?
qa.answer: Beta = 0: slave trade has no long-run effect on current income

equations.label: eq:iv-model
equations.latex: \log S_i = \pi_0 + \pi_1 \log D_i^{coast} + X_i'\pi_2 + \nu_i, \quad \log y_i = \alpha + \beta \widehat{\log S_i} + X_i'\gamma + \varepsilon_i
equations.description: Two-stage least squares: first stage uses distance to coast $D_i^{coast}$ to instrument slave exports; second stage uses predicted exports.

equations.label: eq:gravity-slave
equations.description: Gravity model of slave trade flows: bilateral trade $T_{ij}$ from origin $i$ to destination $j$ depends on distance $D_{ij}$, demand $Y_j$, and price $P_j$.

equations.label: eq:historical-channel
equations.latex: \log y_i = \alpha + \hat{\beta}_D \log \bar{S}_i + \hat{\beta}_I \text{EF}_i + \hat{\beta}_T \text{Trust}_i + X_i'\gamma + \varepsilon_i, \quad \frac{\partial \log y_i}{\partial \log S_i} = \hat{\beta}_D + \hat{\beta}_I \frac{\partial \text{EF}_i}{\partial \log S_i} + \hat{\beta}_T \frac{\partial \text{Trust}_i}{\partial \log S_i}
equations.description: Mechanism equation: slave trade affects income through ethnic fractionalization (EF) and social trust. $\beta_D$ is the direct effect; $\beta_I \cdot \partial\text{EF}/\partial S$ and $\beta_T \cdot \partial\text{Trust}/\partial S$ are indirect channels.

equations.label: eq:ethnic-frac
equations.description: Herfindahl-based ethnic fractionalization index: $s_{ik}$ is the population share of ethnic group $k$ in country $i$.

equations.label: eq:trust-formation
equations.description: Intergenerational trust formation: current trust depends on lagged trust, ethnic fractionalization, and conflict history.

qa.question: What parameter captures intergenerational persistence of trust?
qa.answer: rho_1, the coefficient on lagged trust

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: IV using distance to coast as instrument for slave trade intensity, exploiting the fact that coastal proximity increased slave export exposure

qa.question: What is the 2SLS coefficient on log slaves exported for log GDP per capita?
Why is the IV estimate larger than OLS?
qa.answer: Consistent with attenuation bias from measurement error in slave trade data: IV corrects downward bias in OLS

qa.question: What fraction of the income effect operates through ethnic fractionalization?
qa.answer: Approximately 69 percent of the total effect

qa.question: What is the first-stage F-statistic?
qa.answer: 94.1 with one instrument, well above conventional weak-instrument threshold of 10
